mission: Use the isolated-horizon quantum boundary literature to reduce the last live positive Q5 route from a generic Abelian twist to its exact puncture-U(1) realization target.

theorem_1_external_puncture_u1_structure.statement: In the isolated-horizon quantization literature, the intrinsic horizon geometry is described by a U(1) Chern-Simons connection on a punctured 2-sphere, flat away from the punctures with distributional curvature concentrated at the punctures. So the only externally supported Abelian topological route is a punctured-sphere U(1) line-bundle/holonomy sector.
theorem_1_external_puncture_u1_structure.exact_external_object: U(1) Chern-Simons connection on punctured S^2

theorem_2_current_io_gap.statement: The current IO stack does not derive that puncture-resolved U(1) sector. Paper 19 still leaves the full puncture-resolved horizon algebra open, and the admitted fermionic bridge carrier is built only from the reduced S^2 spinor bundle plus multiplicity factors.

theorem_3_exact_final_missing_data.status: derived / strongest exact current frontier reduction
theorem_3_exact_final_missing_data.statement: The last live positive Q5 route is not a generic Abelian twist. It is the following exact pair of missing data: a puncture-resolved U(1) horizon line bundle L_punct with nonzero degree, and a theorem-grade charged spin^c lift coupling the fermionic bridge sector to that line bundle. Equivalently, the exact missing integer is n = q_chi c1(L_punct)[S^2] ≠ 0.
exact_missing_objects: L_punct: derived puncture-U(1) horizon line bundle
q_chi: derived fermionic charge/spin^c coupling of the bridge sector to L_punct

final_boundary.status: derived / strongest current Paper 35 exact route reduction
final_boundary.safe_statement: Paper 35 is now reduced as far as the current stack and standard isolated-horizon support permit. The only live positive Q5 route is a derived puncture-U(1) line bundle plus a derived charged spin^c coupling into the fermionic bridge. Without those, baryogenesis remains closed on the current stack.
